2604.05297.title: Breakthrough the Suboptimal Stable Point in Value-Factorization-Based Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
2604.05297.paper_summary: Value factorization, a popular paradigm in MARL, faces significant theoretical and algorithmic bottlenecks: its tendency to converge to suboptimal solutions remains poorly understood and unsolved. Theoretically, existing analyses fail to explain this due to their primary focus on the optimal case. To bridge this gap, we introduce a novel theoretical concept: the stable point, which characterizes the potential convergence of value factorization in general cases. Through an analysis of stable point distributions in existing methods, we reveal that non-optimal stable points are the primary cause of poor performance. However, algorithmically, making the optimal action the unique stable point is nearly infeasible. In contrast, iteratively filtering suboptimal actions by rendering them unstable emerges as a more practical approach for global optimality. Inspired by this, we propose a novel Multi-Round Value Factorization (MRVF) framework. Specifically, by measuring a non-negative payoff increment relative to the previously selected action, MRVF transforms inferior actions into unstable points, thereby driving each iteration toward a stable point with a superior action. Experiments on challenging benchmarks, including predator-prey tasks and StarCraft II Multi-Agent Challenge (SMAC), validate our analysis of stable points and demonstrate the superiority of MRVF over state-of-the-art methods.
2604.05297.paper_summary_zh: 價值分解，作為多智能體強化學習（MARL）中的一個流行範式，面臨著重大的理論和算法瓶頸：其收斂到次優解的傾向仍然缺乏充分的理解和解決方案。從理論上講，現有的分析未能解釋這一點，因為它們主要集中於最佳情況。為了彌補這一空白，我們引入了一個新穎的理論概念：穩定點，該概念描述了價值分解在一般情況下的潛在收斂性。通過分析現有方法中的穩定點分佈，我們揭示了非最佳穩定點是性能不佳的主要原因。然而，在算法上，將最佳行動設定為唯一的穩定點幾乎是不可行的。相反，通過使次優行動不穩定來迭代過濾它們，成為實現全局最優的一種更實際的方法。受到這一點的啟發，我們提出了一個新穎的多輪價值分解（MRVF）框架。具體而言，通過測量相對於先前選擇行動的非負收益增量，MRVF將劣質行動轉化為不穩定點，從而推動每次迭代朝著具有優越行動的穩定點前進。在挑戰性基準測試上進行的實驗，包括捕食者-獵物任務和《星際爭霸 II 多智能體挑戰》（SMAC），驗證了我們對穩定點的分析，並展示了MRVF相對於最先進方法的優越性。
2604.05297.author: Lesong Tao et.al.
2604.05297.authors: Lesong Tao, Yifei Wang, Haodong Jing, Jingwen Fu, Miao Kang, Shitao Chen, Nanning Zheng
